name: Delaware Bay
description:
Delaware Bay is a major estuary on the Atlantic coast where the Delaware River meets the ocean, providing exceptional fishing along Delaware's eastern shore. The bay's productive waters support diverse species throughout the year.

Striped bass and weakfish are the primary spring targets, followed by flounder and bluefish in summer. The annual horseshoe crab spawn attracts massive baitfish concentrations that drive spectacular fishing. Multiple public ramps along the bay shore provide access from various points.

name: Indian River Bay
description:
Indian River Bay is a shallow coastal bay in southern Delaware connected to the Atlantic Ocean through Indian River Inlet. The bay's warm, shallow waters are among the most productive fishing grounds in the Mid-Atlantic region.

Flounder are the signature species, with the bay and inlet producing quality fish throughout summer. The Indian River Inlet jetties are legendary fishing spots for striped bass, bluefish, and flounder. The bay's grass flats hold weakfish, croaker, and small bluefish. Several public launches provide access throughout the bay.

name: Rehoboth Bay
description:
Rehoboth Bay is a shallow coastal lagoon behind the popular beach resort towns of Rehoboth and Dewey Beach. Despite its modest depth, the bay supports productive fishing in a family-friendly setting.

Flounder fishing is the main attraction, with anglers drifting live minnows and squid strips over the sandy bottom. The bay's seagrass beds hold croaker and spot throughout summer, providing excellent light-tackle action. Public launch ramps provide easy access for small boats and kayaks.

name: Delaware River
description:
The Delaware River forms the entire eastern boundary of the state, providing tidal and freshwater fishing opportunities from Wilmington to Delaware City. The river's tidal reach supports an impressive mix of species.

Striped bass fishing is productive from spring through fall, with fish migrating through the river. Channel and blue catfish have become increasingly abundant, with some exceeding 30 pounds. The spring shad run provides exciting light-tackle fishing. Several state and municipal ramps provide access along the river.

name: Lums Pond
description:
Lums Pond is Delaware's largest freshwater pond, located in Lums Pond State Park south of the Chesapeake and Delaware Canal. The pond offers the best freshwater fishing in northern Delaware.

Largemouth bass are the premier species, with fish regularly exceeding 5 pounds in the pond's lily pad fields and submerged timber. Crappie and bluegill provide excellent panfishing, especially in spring. A boat launch within the state park provides access, with electric motors only to maintain the pond's peaceful character.

name: Killens Pond
description:
Killens Pond is a popular fishing destination in Killens Pond State Park near Felton in central Delaware. Though small, the pond is well-managed and consistently produces quality warmwater fish.

Largemouth bass up to 6 pounds are caught regularly from the pond's vegetated shoreline. The park's family-friendly atmosphere makes it an ideal spot to introduce young anglers to fishing. A small boat launch accommodates car-top boats and canoes, with electric motors only.
